_comment: Per-camera lens distortion parameters used by the registration-time CCTV
Keyed by Room.stream_key (lowercase, matches mediamtx path).
Distortion model: OpenCV's standard radial+tangential 5-coefficient model.
k1, k2, k3 are radial; p1, p2 are tangential. Negative k1 = barrel (wide-angle).
Positive k1 = pincushion (telephoto). Most security cameras are barrel.
Values below are SPEC-DERIVED APPROXIMATIONS. For production deployment, run
to compute the actual coefficients from a printed checkerboard target. The
approximations here are good enough to materially improve recognition over
no per-camera distortion, but they are not perfect — calibrated values can
lift recognition another ~5-10% in match similarity.
- Reolink CX810 spec sheet: 95° horizontal FOV, 8 MP sensor
- Reolink P340 spec sheet: ~110° horizontal FOV, 12 MP sensor
- k1/k2/k3 estimated from FOV using the Brown-Conrady distortion approximation

eb226.comment: Normal lens, mild barrel distortion. Reolink stock white-balance reads slightly cooler than phone selfies.

eb227.comment: Wide-angle lens, noticeable barrel distortion. Faces near frame edges visibly stretched.

default.comment: Fallback profile when a stream_key has no entry above. Conservative middle-of-the-road values.
